mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) indicates a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both series decline concurrently, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, an increase in the federal funds rate combined with rising core inflation (CPIAUCSL) forces aggressive liquidity withdrawal, which disproportionately penalizes duration-sensitive equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when both the funds rate and CPI rise concurrently during a tightening phase, or the signal's predictive power vanishes once inflation expectations are controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts serves as a leading indicator of broader consumer confidence and construction sector health, mechanically supporting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or housing starts growth shows no statistical correlation with subsequent 5-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls below 3.8%, it signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately capping equity upside and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The market rallies significantly (>2% over 20 days) following dual labor market strength, or the joint condition fails to produce a statistically significant negative return differential.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while the federal funds rate remains elevated indicates that supply-side resilience is outpacing monetary restraint, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sharp rebound in real personal savings (RSAFS) coupled with steady employment growth (PAYEMS) fuels a consumption-driven earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain flat or negative despite the savings and employment rebound, or the signal's predictive power disappears when adjusted for seasonal labor adjustments.
